refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions) clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and compare against customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement robust error correction and retransmission protocols.
DP2: Use adaptive bitrate and compression algorithms to optimize data flow.
DP3: Integrate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP3: Integrate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network monitoring and teleoperation control modules.
Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: DP3 logically encapsulates monitoring and control, minimizing cross-module dependencies.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of delayed feedback causing packet loss spikes.
Assumes availability of accurate telemetry data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms if network monitoring fails or is delayed.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, network monitoring, and communication quality management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time network and system status data to the Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends commands and control signals to the Vehicle module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services in case of communication degradation impacting safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation-specific logic.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical control, less suitable for communication quality management.
Highest containment score; centralizes teleoperation communication logic minimizing diffusion.
Handles safety alerts but not core teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing communication quality management within the Teleoperation module reduces diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module responsibilities.
Dependency on accurate and timely telemetry data from other modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor Teleoperation module complexity and consider splitting monitoring and control if diffusion increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and performance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Possible complexity concentration in Teleoperation module.
Interface dependencies on multiple external modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary to minimize diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FR to DP and IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback or redundancy strategies in DP to mitigate risks of delayed feedback.
Review Teleoperation module complexity in future decomposition levels.

decision.justification: While the decomposition is sound, risks related to Teleoperation module complexity and fallback mechanisms warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant modules for corrective action.

revise.why: Incorporate fallback mechanisms and clarify adaptive control strategies to mitigate risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
open_risks: Delayed feedback causing packet loss spikes.
Potential overload of Teleoperation module responsibilities.
evidence_needed: Performance data from network monitoring under realistic conditions.
